# open-wispr

> Free, open-source voice dictation for macOS. Powered by Whisper AI, runs 100% on-device.

open-wispr is a lightweight macOS menu bar app that provides push-to-talk voice dictation. Press Shift+Space, speak, press again to stop -- transcribed text appears at your cursor in any app. It uses OpenAI's Whisper model running locally via whisper.cpp, so no audio or text ever leaves the machine.

This fork adds a floating on-screen indicator, word tracking stats, and hotkey reliability fixes.

## Key Facts

- **Price**: Free and open source (MIT license)
- **Platform**: macOS 13+ (Apple Silicon required)
- **Privacy**: 100% on-device. Zero network requests after initial model download.
- **Source**: https://github.com/1shanpanta/open-wispr (fork of human37/open-wispr)

## Features

- Works in any app (text editors, browsers, Slack, Terminal, etc.)
- Floating draggable indicator shows recording/transcribing state
- Word tracking: today, this week, all time (menu bar + CLI)
- Multiple Whisper model sizes (tiny, base, small, medium, large)
- Configurable hotkey, language, and model
- Toggle mode or hold-to-talk mode

## System Requirements

- macOS 13 (Ventura) or later
- Apple Silicon (M1+)
- Homebrew installed
- ~150 MB disk space for the default Whisper model
- Microphone and Accessibility permissions

---

## LLM Setup Instructions

Copy everything below this line into your LLM (Claude, ChatGPT, etc.) to set up open-wispr as a macOS application. Run each command and confirm it succeeds before moving to the next step.

### Step 1: Clone and build

```bash
git clone https://github.com/1shanpanta/open-wispr.git ~/Desktop/Code/Passion-Projects/open-wispr
cd ~/Desktop/Code/Passion-Projects/open-wispr
brew install whisper-cpp
swift build -c release
```

Verify the binary exists:

```bash
ls -la .build/release/open-wispr
```

### Step 2: Create the macOS .app bundle

Create the directory structure:

```bash
mkdir -p "/Applications/Open Wispr.app/Contents/MacOS"
mkdir -p "/Applications/Open Wispr.app/Contents/Resources"
```

### Step 3: Create the launcher script

The binary requires the `start` argument. The .app needs a wrapper script that passes it. Write this file to `/Applications/Open Wispr.app/Contents/MacOS/open-wispr-launcher`:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
exec "$HOME/Desktop/Code/Passion-Projects/open-wispr/.build/release/open-wispr" start
```

Then make it executable:

```bash
chmod +x "/Applications/Open Wispr.app/Contents/MacOS/open-wispr-launcher"
```

Important: if the repo is cloned to a different path, update the path in the launcher script.

### Step 4: Create Info.plist

Write this file to `/Applications/Open Wispr.app/Contents/Info.plist`:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
    <key>CFBundleExecutable</key>
    <string>open-wispr-launcher</string>
    <key>CFBundleIdentifier</key>
    <string>com.human37.open-wispr.launcher</string>
    <key>CFBundleName</key>
    <string>Open Wispr</string>
    <key>CFBundleDisplayName</key>
    <string>Open Wispr</string>
    <key>CFBundleVersion</key>
    <string>0.34.0</string>
    <key>CFBundleShortVersionString</key>
    <string>0.34.0</string>
    <key>CFBundlePackageType</key>
    <string>APPL</string>
    <key>LSMinimumSystemVersion</key>
    <string>13.0</string>
    <key>CFBundleIconFile</key>
    <string>AppIcon</string>
    <key>LSUIElement</key>
    <true/>
    <key>NSMicrophoneUsageDescription</key>
    <string>Open Wispr needs microphone access to record speech for transcription.</string>
</dict>
</plist>
```

LSUIElement is true so the app runs as a menu bar app with no dock icon.

### Step 5: Copy the app icon

```bash
cp ~/Desktop/Code/Passion-Projects/open-wispr/Resources/AppIcon.icns "/Applications/Open Wispr.app/Contents/Resources/"
```

### Step 6: Register with macOS

This makes the app discoverable by Spotlight and Raycast:

```bash
/System/Library/Frameworks/CoreServices.framework/Frameworks/LaunchServices.framework/Support/lsregister -f "/Applications/Open Wispr.app"
```

### Step 7: Launch

```bash
open "/Applications/Open Wispr.app"
```

### Step 8: Add to PATH

Symlink the binary so `open-wispr` works from any terminal:

```bash
ln -sf ~/Desktop/Code/Passion-Projects/open-wispr/.build/release/open-wispr /opt/homebrew/bin/open-wispr
```

If using fish shell, rehash so it picks up the new command:

```bash
fish -c "fish_rehash" 2>/dev/null; hash -r 2>/dev/null
```

Verify it works (note the hyphen -- it's `open-wispr` not `openwispr`):

```bash
open-wispr stats
```

### Step 9: Grant permissions

macOS will prompt for:
1. **Microphone access** -- allow it
2. **Accessibility access** -- go to System Settings > Privacy & Security > Accessibility and enable open-wispr

### Verification

After setup, confirm these work:

1. Search "Open Wispr" in Raycast or Spotlight -- it should appear and launch
2. A floating circle indicator appears on screen (drag it anywhere)
3. Click the menu bar waveform icon -- you should see word stats (Today / This Week / All Time)
4. Press Shift+Space to start recording, press again to stop
5. Run `~/Desktop/Code/Passion-Projects/open-wispr/.build/release/open-wispr stats` to see word counts in terminal

### Updating

After pulling new changes:

```bash
cd ~/Desktop/Code/Passion-Projects/open-wispr
git pull
swift build -c release
pkill -f "open-wispr start"
open "/Applications/Open Wispr.app"
```

The launcher script always points to the latest binary. No need to recreate the .app bundle.

### Configuration

Edit `~/.config/open-wispr/config.json`:

```json
{
  "hotkey": { "keyCode": 49, "modifiers": ["shift"] },
  "modelSize": "base.en",
  "language": "en",
  "spokenPunctuation": false,
  "maxRecordings": 0,
  "toggleMode": true
}
```

Common hotkey codes: Space (49), Globe (63), Right Option (61), F5 (96).
Modifiers: "cmd", "ctrl", "shift", "opt".
